titleStmt.title: SII 28.269: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 269. (A.R. No. 423 of 1913.) ĀṞAGAḶŪR, ATTUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.

summary.p: Kāmēśvara temple, central shrine, south base of the maṇḍapa in front.

The stone containing the record is seen to have been cut into fragments. Only four fragments are available. Fortunately, these four fragments constitute two sets (i.e.) two fragments in each set giving a continuous sense. The beginning of the inscription is however lost.

In the light of the contents found in the preceding record (No. 422), it can be understood that this is a pledge given by Irumaḍi Irāyappa-nāyakkar Timmaiyanāyakkar to the nāṭṭavar of Magadai-maṇḍalam to put an end to the practice of collecting oppressive levies (aṭantēṟṟam) and moderate the rate of the levies like kāl-vāśi, kai-vilai-dhāṉiyam, three kinds of vartiṉai, vāra-ppaṟṟu, kaṇ-kūli etc.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
mātam 3vatu nāḷ śrīmatu Irumaṭi Irāyappa nāyakkar timmaiya nāyakkar makatai maṇṭalam nāṭṭavaṟku kalveṭṭik kuṭuttapaṭi
yāle Aṭanteṟṟam Ākakkoṇṭa kālvāci kaiy vilai tāṉiyam Aṉuvaṟtiṉai kovaivaṟtiṉai Atikāravaṟtiṉai palavum vaṟtiṉai koḷḷu
takkatākavum kaṟpittu Ūrkkālvāci pūṟuvam Aṟṟa mariāti mutal mutal kālukkup paṭikkāl kalattukku kuṟuṇi nāṉāḻi Uḷvaṭṭam kūṭṭā
ḷātapaṭiyum kaṟpittu vārappaṟṟu nellu Uḷḷatu koṭṭārak kaṭṭāka Aḷakkumpaṭiyum kaṟpittu kāṇippaṟṟu ne
Two more connected payments of the same record as above.

kaḻittu vaṭṭam kaṇkūlikku nūṟṟukku Oru paṇam koḷḷat takkatākavum kaṟpittu kāṇikkai ka
yāti kaḻittu kallum veṭṭik kuṭuttom Immariātikku Inta vari Uṇṭāṉa
yakkar Āṇaiye tappātu Āṉa Aḷavukku Iyppaṭikku Iṉava Irumaṭināyakka timmaiyanāyakkar Eḻuttu §
Digital edition of SII 28.269 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
